verbatim_text: And so what we did in ML Gym, and this was my main contribution here that ended up being deployed at Meta, was we used performance profiles. Performance profiles are a thing that exists. And I didn't invent performance profiles, but they have been used in things like optimization and performance modeling for a long time. The idea here is that you've got some curve that tells you the percentage of tasks for which your method is within some range of the state of the art. In this range, this amount tau is this x-axis here. And basically a higher curve is better. If your curve is all the way to one the whole time, that means your model is the state of the art on all of the tasks you evaluated it on. And so most curves are not going to look like that. They're not going to be state of the art on everything. And so in general, a higher curve is better. So my contribution here was to turn this into a single score because this is actually a curve and it's hard to compare things like that.
